_comment_lanes: standup has three lanes. The local git lane is always on once repoRoots is set (no account needed). The host lane (provider) and board lane (board) are opt-in.
_comment_git: LOCAL GIT LANE — the only lane you need to be useful. repoRoots are directories scanned for git repos (a root that is itself a repo is included; otherwise its immediate subdirectories are scanned one level deep). gitAuthor matches commits by name or email; empty uses each repo's own git config user.email.
_comment_host: HOST/PR LANE — set provider to pull in the PRs you opened/merged/reviewed. 'github' uses the gh CLI (run `gh auth login` once). 'azure' is a documented stub until it is built on a machine with az access. 'none' turns the lane off (the default: the git lane alone is already useful).
_comment_azure: For provider=azure (stub for now): repos live under an org/project. These are documented so a work-machine session can implement providers/azure.js against the same neutral contract.
_comment_board: BOARD/TICKET LANE — set board to pull in tickets that moved or are in progress. 'jira' is a documented stub until it is built (Jira has no ubiquitous CLI; the adapter uses the REST API + an API token). 'none' turns the lane off.
_comment_window: lookbackHours is how far back 'since your last standup' reaches (48 covers a weekend Monday).
_comment_flags: The third column. flagReviewHours: an open PR waiting this long for a review is flagged. flagStuckDays: an in-progress ticket unmoved this long is flagged.
_comment_ai: OPT-IN AI WRITE-UP. Set claudeExe to your claude CLI path to enable the one-click 'write it up' button, which compresses the raw events into a few spoken-style bullets. Empty = the feature is off and the board is fully useful without it.
